Hexadigitall Academy (Hexadigitall Technologies)
www.hexadigitall.com
Scan to view the course page, enrollment options, and mentorship details.

Course Snapshot

Design user-centric digital products. Master Figma, wireframing, prototyping, and user research.

Product Design (UI/UX) Bootcamp

A professionally structured weekly curriculum aligned to the level, tooling, and delivery expectations of this course.

Duration: 12 Weeks
Level: Beginner
Study Time: 5 hours/week + labs

Welcome to Product Design (UI/UX) Bootcamp! 🎓

This curriculum is designed to take you from core understanding to confident delivery through weekly applied practice, measurable outcomes, and portfolio evidence.

Each week builds progressively with practical tasks, implementation checkpoints, and reflection points so you can convert knowledge into repeatable professional performance.

Your success is our priority. Stay consistent with weekly execution, document your work, and use feedback loops to continuously improve your delivery quality.

Prerequisites

  • Visual hierarchy fundamentals: contrast, alignment, whitespace, and intentional typography choices
  • Color theory application: psychology, accessibility standards, and palette cohesiveness
  • Wireframing and prototyping experience with tools like Figma, Adobe XD, or Sketch
  • Understanding of responsive design, breakpoints, and mobile-first composition strategies

Learning Outcomes

  • Master Figma for wireframing and prototyping.
  • Conduct user research and create user personas.
  • Understand design systems, typography, and color theory.
  • Build a professional design portfolio.

Recommended Complementary Courses

  • Pair this curriculum with a related foundation or advanced specialization to strengthen adjacent skill areas.
  • Select one systems-focused and one delivery-focused course to improve both implementation depth and execution speed.
  • Use complementary study tracks to broaden portfolio evidence and improve interview and project readiness.

Essential Learning Resources

  • Design system templates, component libraries, and accessibility audit checklists
  • Figma starter kits with typography scales, color systems, and responsive breakpoints
  • Usability testing scripts and design critique frameworks

Your Learning Roadmap

Foundation

Weeks 1-3

  • Early Weeks: Design fundamentals, visual hierarchy, and component basics
  • Middle Weeks: Design systems, accessibility standards, and interaction patterns
  • Late Weeks: Portfolio building, stakeholder communication, and design leadership

Build

Weeks 4-6

  • Information Architecture and Feature Prioritization
  • Wireframes for Core User Flows
  • High-Fidelity User Interface Design in Figma

Integration

Weeks 7-9

  • Components, Variants, and Design Systems
  • Interactive Prototypes and Micro-Interactions
  • Usability Testing and Design Iteration

Capstone

Weeks 10-12

  • Handoff Specifications and Developer Collaboration
  • Portfolio Case Study Structure and Storytelling
  • Capstone: End-to-End Product Design Case Study

Detailed Weekly Curriculum

Week 1 5 hours/week + labs
Product Discovery and Problem Definition
  • Identify the principles of Product Discovery and Problem Definition and link them to course outcomes in time-boxed sprints with rapid feedback loops.
  • Explain Product Discovery and Problem Definition in a guided scenario using realistic tools, constraints, and quality gates.
  • Apply trade-offs, risks, and decision points for Product Discovery and Problem Definition, then record rationale for stakeholder review.
  • Document a portfolio-ready design system case study for Product Discovery and Problem Definition with measurable success criteria and next actions.
  • Track measurable progress using rubric scores, defect/risk trends, and evidence completeness each week.
  • Run a short retrospective focused on what to retain, improve, and scale into the following week.
  • Incorporate peer or mentor feedback and revise the week deliverable to professional publication quality.
  • Publish the week output into your cumulative portfolio with concise outcome narrative and proof artifacts.
Week 2 5 hours/week + labs
User Research Planning and Interview Synthesis
  • Identify the principles of User Research Planning and Interview Synthesis and link them to course outcomes in time-boxed sprints with rapid feedback loops.
  • Explain User Research Planning and Interview Synthesis in a guided scenario using realistic tools, constraints, and quality gates.
  • Apply trade-offs, risks, and decision points for User Research Planning and Interview Synthesis, then record rationale for stakeholder review.
  • Document a portfolio-ready design system case study for User Research Planning and Interview Synthesis with measurable success criteria and next actions.
  • Track measurable progress using rubric scores, defect/risk trends, and evidence completeness each week.
  • Run a short retrospective focused on what to retain, improve, and scale into the following week.
  • Incorporate peer or mentor feedback and revise the week deliverable to professional publication quality.
  • Publish the week output into your cumulative portfolio with concise outcome narrative and proof artifacts.
Week 3 5 hours/week + labs

Week 5 5 hours/week + labs

  • Evaluate trade-offs, risks, and decision points for Wireframes for Core User Flows, then record rationale for stakeholder review.

Week 6 5 hours/week + labs

Capstone: End-to-End Product Design Case Study
  • Analyze the principles of Capstone: End-to-End Product Design Case Study and link them to course outcomes in time-boxed sprints with rapid feedback loops.
  • Evaluate Capstone: End-to-End Product Design Case Study in a guided scenario using realistic tools, constraints, and quality gates.
  • Create trade-offs, risks, and decision points for Capstone: End-to-End Product Design Case Study, then record rationale for stakeholder review.
  • Defend a portfolio-ready design system case study for Capstone: End-to-End Product Design Case Study with measurable success criteria and next actions.
  • Track measurable progress using rubric scores, defect/risk trends, and evidence completeness each week.
  • Run a short retrospective focused on what to retain, improve, and scale into the following week.
  • Incorporate peer or mentor feedback and revise the week deliverable to professional publication quality.
  • Publish the week output into your cumulative portfolio with concise outcome narrative and proof artifacts.

Capstone Projects

Project 1: Research-to-Wireframe Validation Sprint

Conduct targeted user research, synthesize findings, and deliver validated wireframes for a core product flow.

  • Interview and synthesis evidence pack
  • Annotated wireframes with rationale
  • Prioritized usability risk list

Project 2: Design System and Prototype Expansion

Build a reusable component and token system, then apply it to an interactive multi-screen prototype with realistic state coverage.

  • Component and variant library in Figma
  • Interactive prototype with key user journeys
  • Iteration log from usability feedback

Project 3: Capstone: End-to-End Product Design Case Study

Deliver a portfolio-grade product design case study from discovery through final handoff, including measurable outcomes and technical collaboration notes.

  • Complete process documentation and artifacts
  • Final high-fidelity prototype and handoff specs
  • Professional presentation and defense narrative

Study Tips

  • Allocate two weekly blocks for design critique, usability testing observations, and component refinement.
  • Maintain a component inventory tracking reuse rates, accessibility audit findings, and visual consistency improvements.
  • Conduct weekly design reviews measuring against accessibility standards (WCAG 2.1), performance impact, and engineering feasibility.

Study Tips for Success

  • Protect consistent weekly practice time and complete hands-on work before moving to the next topic.
  • Document implementation decisions, trade-offs, and lessons learned after each weekly deliverable.
  • Review feedback quickly and ship an improved revision within the same week to reinforce retention.
  • Track measurable progress with checklists, test evidence, and milestone outcomes.

About Product Design (UI/UX) Bootcamp

This curriculum is structured to build practical capability, consistent delivery discipline, and portfolio-ready outcomes in Product Design (UI/UX) Bootcamp. It combines conceptual understanding with applied execution so learners can perform confidently in real project environments.

© 2026 Hexadigitall Academy (Hexadigitall Technologies). Curriculum authored for Product Design (UI/UX) Bootcamp.
